title: Amperometric Study on Vanadates of Thallium(I) and its Determination as Pyrovanadate
author: Ram Sahai Saxena
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The stoichiometry of the compounds, precipitated by the interaction of thallous salt and different alkali vanadates (ortho, pyro, and meta), has been investigated by means of amperometric titrations between the reagents. The end paints, obtained from titration curves, provide definite evidence for the formation and precipitation of thallous pyro- and meta-vanadates at  p H ranges of 8.4 to 8.6 and 6.1 to 6.4 respectively. The precipitation of thallous pyrovanadate has been found to be quantitative. The amperometric results are aceurate and reproducible; hence the titration has been developed as an analytical method for the determination of thallium(I) in solutions.
steps_raw: The stoichiometry of the compounds, precipitated by the interaction of thallous salt and different alkali vanadates (ortho, pyro, and meta), has been investigated by means of amperometric titrations between the reagents. The end paints, obtained from titration curves, provide definite evidence for the formation and precipitation of thallous pyro- and meta-vanadates at  p H ranges of 8.4 to 8.6 and 6.1 to 6.4 respectively. The precipitation of thallous pyrovanadate has been found to be quantitative. The amperometric results are aceurate and reproducible; hence the titration has been developed as an analytical method for the determination of thallium(I) in solutions.
